title: PHP Form Handling
content:
### PHP Form Handling

PHP is excellent for processing HTML form data sent via GET or POST methods.

### Simple Form

```php
<form method="post" action="<?php echo htmlspecialchars($_SERVER["PHP_SELF"]); ?>">
    Name: <input type="text" name="name">
    <input type="submit">
</form>

<?php
if ($_SERVER["REQUEST_METHOD"] == "POST") {
    $name = htmlspecialchars($_POST["name"]);
    echo "Hello, $name";
}
?>
```

### Form Attributes

| Attribute | Value | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `method` | `get` or `post` | HTTP method |
| `action` | URL | Where to send form data |
| `enctype` | `multipart/form-data` | For file uploads |

### GET vs POST

| Feature | GET | POST |
|---------|-----|------|
| Data location | URL parameters | Request body |
| Visibility | Visible in URL | Hidden |
| Bookmarkable | Yes | No |
| Size limit | ~2000 chars | Much larger |
| Security | Not secure | More secure |
| File uploads | No | Yes |

> **Key Point:** Use `$_SERVER["REQUEST_METHOD"]` to check the form method. Always use `htmlspecialchars()` when outputting form data to prevent XSS attacks.
